event: warmed Hartmann's one litre infusion commenced
undisplaced fracture of his left acetabulum of both the anterior and posterior columns
referred to the Orthopaedic on-call team after an initial delay
blood pressure dropped to 75/51 mm Hg
intensive care, orthopaedic and general registrar attended the patient
ecchymosis of left lower abdominal quadrant
pelvic binder in place but not tightened
noradrenaline infusion to maintain MAP > 65 mm Hg
transfused 2 adult therapeutic doses of platelets
CT scan deemed necessary for diagnosis and management planning
CT abdomen confirmed fracture of the left acetabulum
significant haematoma on left pelvic sidewall adjacent to the fracture
8 mm enhancing nodule suggestive of internal iliac artery pseudoaneurysm
ill-defined blush of contrast within the haematoma
extraperitoneal haematoma inseparable from the bladder
transferred to the intensive care unit
CT angiogram demonstrated no arterial bleed
gelfoam embolization of anterior and posterior division segmental branches of the left internal iliac artery
developed uncontrollable bleeding from pin site
continuous pressure applied to the wound
transferred to gastroenterology after orthopaedic management due to severity of liver disease
cause of death was chronic liver disease
